title: Celtics reportedly sign Ron Harper Jr. to 3-year deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to a three-year, $9 million contract, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Boston declined Harper's $2.6 million team option for the 2026-27 season to offer a longer-term deal.
Harper, 26, averaged 4.0 points, 1.7 rebounds over 26 games, with strong shooting performance last season.

title: 2 JP residents bitten by raccoons in separate incidents, animal control says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Jamaica Plain residents were bitten by raccoons in separate incidents over the weekend, officials reported.
Both people were treated at hospitals; animal control has yet to locate the involved raccoons.
Authorities advise avoiding contact with wild animals and keeping pets vaccinated against rabies.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Norway-France World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick for France in their 4-1 win over Norway at Gillette Stadium.
A crowd of over 64,000 witnessed the Norway-France World Cup match, featuring notable performances by both teams.
Kylian Mbappe contributed two assists for France, despite being kept off the scoreboard in the Foxborough game.

title: 5 prospects to watch during Bruins Development Camp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins Development Camp runs from June 29 to July 2 at Warrior Ice Arena with nearly 30 players.
Dean Letourneau is a standout prospect following a successful sophomore year at Boston College with 22 goals.
Swedish goalie Max Lundgren joins the camp after a strong season with Merrimack, helping them win a Hockey East title.

title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A duck boat overturned at 10 Museum Way in Cambridge, injuring 11, including one seriously, on Saturday.
The accident occurred while the vehicle was being towed after a mechanical issue; the tow rope snapped, causing it to tip.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations Saturday, and the vessel was removed at 7:30 p.m.; investigations continue.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins traded two first-round picks to acquire 24-year-old winger JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth.
Peterka has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years with Buffalo and Utah.
Addition of Peterka aims to boost Bruins' top-six scoring and speed, aligning with their strategic needs.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth to halt coverage of GLP-1 and anti-obesity drugs for weight loss starting July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change, part of Gov. Healey's fiscal 2027 budget, aims to save $15 million annually despite rising healthcare costs.
Drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound will still be covered for conditions like Type 2 diabetes; direct purchase costs $350-$500 monthly.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins selected seven players over rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, including two goalies and several wingers.
Second-round pick Yuri Ivanov, a goalie, is a long-term project expected to stay in Russia for two more years.
Sixth-round selection Roberto Henriquez, a goalie, is committed to Boston College and known for his strong footwork and athleticism.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two earthquakes in Venezuela result in 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing, impacting Red Sox players from the country.
Venezuelan Red Sox players express solidarity by writing messages on their hats for earthquake victims.
David Ortiz is raising awareness and funds at Fenway Park for Venezuelan earthquake relief efforts.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown disputed a report suggesting analytics rank him as seventh-best on an NBA team on social media.
Bobby Marks shared that some NBA teams have mixed views on Brown due to analytics, sparking online debate.
Despite analytics criticism, Brown finished top-10 in MVP voting after a career-peak season with the Celtics.
